div.h1: Non-Power-of-Two Tree Handling in vEB Layout

p.strong: Technical Specification for Memory Alignment and Cache Optimization

text.tspan: * Handling Non-Power-of-Two subtrees via Virtual Padding

g.text: inline void* veb_get_child(veb_node_t* base, uint32_t idx) {

text.tspan: // Fast log2 calculation using compiler intrinsics
* Handling Non-Power-of-Two subtrees via Virtual Padding
// Fast log2 calculation using compiler intrinsics

ul.li: Logic: Static index math: 'O(1)' address generation
Pros: No bounds checks in inner loops
Waste: 0 Bytes, but high CPU overhead

ol.li: Small Trees (N < 1024): Use Full Padding. Memory waste is trivial and performance is ~2.4x higher.
Massive Trees (N > 1M): Use Incomplete subtrees at top levels to save GBs, but switch to Full Padding for leaf subtrees to ensure L1 Cache line alignment.
